text.title: Migration Assessment and Field Mapping
text.summary: Pre-migration audit structure, field mapping rules, and status/priority mapping tables for cross-tool PM data migration.

rule.statement: Complete a pre-migration audit before any data movement: count total issues, attachments (by byte), custom fields, automation rules, and integrations; classify each integration as critical or non-critical.
rule.rationale: Skipping the audit produces mid-migration surprises — attachment size limits exceed target storage, custom fields have no target equivalent, critical integrations break with no rollback plan.

example.code: audit_checklist: data_inventory: total_projects: count total_issues: count total_attachments_gb: size custom_fields: list automations: count integrations: list usage_patterns: active_users: count key_workflows: list power_users: list pain_points: list dependencies: ci_cd: [tool, criticality] slack: [channels, automations] documentation_links: [wiki, confluence] compliance: audit_requirements: yes_or_no data_retention: policy access_controls: requirements

rule.statement: Every source field must be explicitly mapped to a target field OR explicitly marked as "won't migrate" with a documented reason; unmapped fields that silently drop data create post-cutover blame cycles.
rule.rationale: Documenting every "won't migrate" decision pre-empts post-cutover disputes about missing data. Silence is not agreement.

rule.statement: Preserve source IDs in a "legacy_id" custom field on the target tool; external systems that hardcoded source issue IDs (commit messages, bookmarks, documentation links) still resolve.
rule.rationale: ID changes (PROJ-1234 → ABC-5) break every external link simultaneously. The legacy_id field is the only safe bridge.

example.code: | Source (Jira) | Target (Linear) | Transformation | Won't Migrate | |------------------|------------------|-----------------------|---------------| | Summary | Title | Direct copy | No | | Description | Description | Markdown convert | No | | Issue Type | Label | Bug→bug, Story→feat | No | | Status | State | See status mapping | No | | Priority | Priority | See priority mapping | No | | Sprint | Cycle | Match by date range | No | | Epic Link | Project | Create Linear projects| No | | Reporter | — | System-managed | Yes — log | | Custom Plugin X | — | No equivalent | Yes — archive |

status_mapping: "Open": "Backlog" "To Do": "Todo" "In Progress": "In Progress" "In Review": "In Review" "Done": "Done" "Won't Do": "Canceled"
priority_mapping: "Highest": "Urgent" "High": "High" "Medium": "Medium" "Low": "Low" "Lowest": "No Priority"
example.note: Status semantics rarely map 1:1. Review each mapping for behavioral differences: "Resolved" in Jira means approved-not-yet-deployed; check whether the target tool has an equivalent state or if items should map to "Done".

text.reference: Source-to-target field mapping table template with ambiguity flags
Post-migration count validation: source vs target with drift threshold
